mainEntity.name: How long does name change take?

acceptedAnswer.text: 15-30 days from board approval to revised COI.

mainEntity.name: How to change a company name in India?

acceptedAnswer.text: File RUN (Reserve Unique Name) on MCA portal for name approval. Pass special resolution at EGM (75% majority). File INC-24 with RoC within 30 days of special resolution. RoC issues new Certificate of Incorporation. Update PAN, TAN, GST, bank accounts, and all statutory registrations.

mainEntity.name: How much does company name change cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Government fee: ₹1,000 for RUN + ₹3,000-₹6,000 for INC-24 (depends on authorised capital). Professional fee: ₹5,000-₹10,000. Timeline: 15-20 working days from RUN approval to new CoI.

mainEntity.name: Is board resolution enough for name change?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Name change requires a special resolution (Section 13) passed at EGM with 75% majority, not just a board resolution. Board resolution is needed to convene the EGM.

mainEntity.name: Can a name change affect existing contracts?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. The company retains the same CIN, PAN, and legal identity. Existing contracts, licences, and obligations continue. Only the name changes on the Certificate of Incorporation.

mainEntity.name: What if the proposed name is rejected by MCA?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can resubmit RUN with a different name (2 names allowed per application). Common rejection reasons: similarity to existing companies, use of restricted words (National, India, Government), or deceptive similarity to well-known brands.
